spacing_and_layout.card_style: Flat background, 1px subtle border, no shadow

surface_styles.header: Solid Background (bg-zinc-950) with hard bottom border (border-zinc-800)
surface_styles.cards: Grid Borders (Technical Look), bg-zinc-900 border border-zinc-800
surface_styles.code_blocks: bg-black border border-zinc-800 text-zinc-300 font-mono text-sm p-4 rounded-sm

components.navigation: Top fixed header with Brand + Links, z-50
components.buttons: Sharp corners or slight radius (rounded-sm), high contrast (bg-white text-black for primary CTA, bg-zinc-800 text-white for secondary), dense padding (px-4 py-2)
components.inputs: Shadcn defaults heavily customized: bg-black border-zinc-800 focus:border-white focus:ring-1 focus:ring-white rounded-sm
components.tables: Minimalist bordered layout, JetBrains Mono for numeric/data cells, text-sm
components.badges: Square or slight round, uppercase mono text for Risk Levels, solid background for high contrast (e.g., bg-red-500 text-white)

motion.hover: Subtle background color shifts, no heavy scaling

specific_instructions_for_main_agent: Force Dark Mode by default. Do not implement a light mode toggle unless explicitly required, to maintain the 'Control Room' / Cybersecurity aesthetic.
Use pure HTML/Tailwind for structural bento/grid layouts, utilizing Shadcn primarily for interactive complex components (Dropdowns, Tabs, Tables).
Implement the Control Room Grid for the main dashboard: 1-col on mobile, 3-col or 4-col widget grids on desktop.
Risk Level Badges (Critical/High/Med/Low) MUST use 'JetBrains Mono' and strict color coding matching the status colors.
No gradients or soft shadows. Use 1px solid borders (border-zinc-800) for all cards and sections to emphasize the 'Technical/Swiss Brutalist' look.
For the Top 5 Ranked Cyber Risks, use a dense list or table. Use monospace fonts for risk scores and metrics.
Data ingested (MDR threat reports, JSON logs, NIST guidelines) should be presented in terminal/code-like output windows using inset containers (bg-black border border-zinc-800).
Use lucide-react for all icons (e.g., ShieldAlert, Database, Server, Activity, ChevronRight).
Ensure all interactive and key informational elements have a 'data-testid' attribute using kebab-case role-based naming (e.g., data-testid='ingest-data-button').
